page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.m1 server family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and runs on the KVM hypervisor. The instance features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, providing a total of 8 vCPUs. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention from hyperthreading.

The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external block storage for operating systems and data persistence. The CPU flags indicate a lack of advanced vector extensions (AVX, AVX-512), matrix extensions (AMX), and hardware-accelerated cryptography (AES) flags in this specific configuration.

Given its balanced resource distribution and dedicated CPU cores, the ecs.m1.xlarge is suited for standard workloads such as web serving, application hosting, and moderate database tasks that do not require specialized hardware acceleration or local NVMe storage. The absence of hyperthreading makes it a predictable choice for workloads sensitive to CPU scheduling latency.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance from the ecs.m1 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated x86_64 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, providing 8 vCPUs without hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio. It contains no local storage or GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. Lacking advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 or AMX, this instance offers a straightforward, predictable compute profile. It is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, application hosting, and standard business applications that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation without requiring specialized hardware accelerators.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 compute instance with a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio for predictable general-purpose workloads without hyperthreading.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per vCPU
